engineIntelligence.h2: Subaru Lucra Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the KF-series engines fitted to the Subaru Lucra in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical replacement costs based on current UK market data for grey import specialists.

cards.altText: Subaru Lucra KF-VE engine - petrol 0.66L replacement UK
knownFailures: Injector failure - causes rough idle, stalling and misfire codes on higher-mileage grey imports.
Coolant sensor connector corrosion - leads to ECU communication errors and overheating warnings.
MAF sensor degradation - causes rich mixture, black spark plugs and jerky acceleration.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £1,500-£2,200 (supply only) Estimated from comparable KF units - actual quotes may vary.

cards.altText: Subaru Lucra KF-DET engine - petrol 0.66L replacement UK
knownFailures: Turbocharger wear - boost loss and oil consumption after 60k miles on enthusiast-driven imports.
Injector failure - high-pressure injectors degrade causing rough running and check engine lights.
Intercooler piping leaks - common on high-mileage units causing boost pressure loss.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £1,800-£2,600 (supply only) Estimated from comparable KF-DET units - actual quotes may vary.

cards.altText: Subaru Lucra KF engine - petrol 0.66L Daihatsu compatible replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear - cold start rattle on units exceeding 70k miles without proper maintenance.
Oil consumption from ring wear - increases after 60k miles on grey imports with incomplete service history.
VVT solenoid wear - causes rough idle and warning lights on higher-mileage Japanese imports.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £1,500-£2,600 (supply only) Estimated from comparable KF units across Daihatsu/Toyota donor pool - actual quotes may vary.

commonProblems.h2: Subaru Lucra Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Subaru Lucra relies solely on the KF-series 0.66L petrol boxer engine across all UK grey import variants. While generally reliable for a kei car, injector failure, coolant sensor corrosion and turbo wear on KF-DET variants dominate replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: KF-VE Injector Failure - Why Subaru Lucra Engines Stall and Misfire
problems.affectedModels: Subaru Lucra Custom, Custom S, Custom RS (2010-2015, KF-VE/KF-DET)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 50,000-80,000 miles on grey imports with incomplete Japanese service history
problems.rootCause: High-pressure fuel injectors degrade from fuel quality variations and infrequent servicing; failed injectors cause misfires, rough idle and stalling.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty injector(s), clear ECU codes, road test adaptation
repairOptions.longevity: Restores smooth running if caught before secondary damage to catalytic converter

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt KF engine with new injectors, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if multiple injectors failed or engine has high mileage; prevents repeat failures

problems.recommendation: On a grey import Lucra worth £2,000-£4,000, if only one injector has failed and mileage is under 70k, injector replacement at £280-£480 is sensible. However, if multiple injectors have failed or mileage exceeds 70k, a rebuilt KF engine at £1,500-£2,200 offers better long-term value with UK warranty coverage.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt KF engine prices for your Subaru Lucra

problems.h4: Coolant Sensor Connector Corrosion - Overheating Risk on Subaru Lucra
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 40,000-70,000 miles, especially on pre-2012 units or imports with poor maintenance
problems.rootCause: Coolant temperature sensor connector corrodes from moisture ingress; degraded connection causes ECU communication errors and false overheating warnings.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace corroded connector, clean terminals, coolant system pressure test
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught early; prevents secondary overheating damage to head gasket

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt KF engine with new sensors, revised gaskets, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Necessary if overheating has already caused head warpage or gasket failure

problems.recommendation: If your Lucra shows only warning lights with no actual overheating, connector repair at £95-£180 is the sensible first step. However, if the engine has already overheated, have a compression test performed - if results are poor, a rebuilt KF at £1,500-£2,200 avoids repeated repair costs.
problems.cta: Find Subaru Lucra cooling system compatible engines

problems.h4: KF-DET Turbo Wear - Boost Loss on Subaru Lucra Custom RS
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 50,000-75,000 miles on enthusiast-driven or poorly maintained grey imports
problems.rootCause: Turbocharger bearings wear from heat cycles and oil starvation; degraded seals cause boost loss, blue smoke and eventual seizure if ignored.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, rebuild or replace with OEM-spec unit, oil feed line inspection
repairOptions.longevity: Restores boost if engine internals undamaged; requires turbo specialist knowledge

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt KF-DET with new turbo, upgraded gaskets, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if turbo failure caused oil contamination or engine damage; peace of mind for turbo owners

problems.recommendation: If your Custom RS shows only boost loss with no knocking or oil contamination, turbo replacement at £420-£750 may suffice. However, if oil contamination occurred or mileage is high, a rebuilt KF-DET at £1,800-£2,600 avoids repeat failures and protects your investment.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt KF-DET turbo engine prices for your Subaru Lucra

problems.h4: MAF Sensor Failure - Rich Mixture on Subaru Lucra KF Engines
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Any mileage if air filter maintenance neglected or sensor contaminated
problems.rootCause: Mass airflow sensor becomes contaminated or electrically degraded; incorrect air measurement causes rich mixture, black spark plugs and failed emissions tests.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace MAF sensor, clear ECU adaptation codes, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Restores correct air-fuel ratio if engine internals sound; effective for sensor-only failures

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt KF engine with new sensors, ECU adaptation service, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Necessary if rich mixture has caused catalytic converter damage or severe carbon buildup

problems.recommendation: If your Lucra shows only MAF-related warning lights with no catalytic converter damage, sensor replacement at £180-£320 is the sensible first step. However, if black spark plugs and rich mixture have persisted, a rebuilt KF at £1,500-£2,200 with fresh sensors avoids repeated emissions test failures.
problems.cta: Compare MOT-compliant Subaru Lucra engine prices

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Subaru Lucra
finalCta.paragraph: The Subaru Lucra's KF-series engines are compact and efficient when maintained, but grey import status, kei car parts scarcity and known failure patterns mean replacements can be challenging. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK specialists who understand Lucra compatibility, chassis codes (L455F/L465F), Daihatsu cross-references and UK compliance. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing original failure points and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare quotes today and get your Lucra back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Subaru Lucra Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Subaru Lucra and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Subaru Lucra Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Subaru Lucra production timeline, organised so grey import owners can quickly identify their KF variant and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Subaru Lucra launched in Japan with KF-series engines across Custom, Custom S and Custom RS trims.
keyChanges: First production year for Subaru Lucra grey imports to UK
L455F (2WD) and L465F (4WD) chassis codes introduced with KF/KF-VE/KF-DET engine options
Early KF calibration with pre-revision injector specifications requiring attention on high-mileage units
mainEngines: KF-VE - 0.66L petrol naturally aspirated (Custom, Custom S)
knownFor: Early KF units may exhibit injector sensitivity; verify service history before purchase
KF-DET turbo requires diligent oil change intervals to prevent bearing wear
Strong donor parts availability via shared Daihatsu Tanto Exe platform
years.cta: Get quotes for 2010 Subaru Lucra replacement engines
years.ticker: Subaru Lucra 2010 engine replacement • L455F KF compatibility • Early KF injector failure

years.description: Mid-production updates introduced revised KF calibration and improved sensor specifications for enhanced reliability.
keyChanges: Revised KF injector specifications to address early failure patterns on high-mileage imports
Updated coolant sensor connector design to reduce corrosion-related ECU errors
Final production year 2015 marked end of Lucra availability for UK grey imports
mainEngines: KF-VE - 0.66L petrol with revised injectors (Custom, Custom S late production)

faq.h2: Subaru Lucra Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Subaru Lucra engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Subaru Lucra engine replacement costs range from £500 for Japanese used imports to £2,600 for UK reconditioned KF-DET turbo units, depending on engine code, variant and supplier. Supply & fit services add £400-£700 labour.
keyPoints: Japanese used KF engines start from £500 with auction grade verification
UK reconditioned KF-VE units range £1,500-£2,200 with 12-month warranty
KF-DET turbo variants typically cost £300-£500 more than naturally aspirated
Supply & fit labour adds £400-£700 depending on specialist location and AWD/CVT complexity

items.question: What engine code fits my Subaru Lucra?
items.answer: All Subaru Lucra variants use the KF-series 0.66L petrol engine code, but chassis codes L455F (2WD) and L465F (4WD) determine compatibility with your vehicle's drivetrain and mounting points.
keyPoints: KF is the dominant engine family code across Custom, Custom S and Custom RS variants
Verify chassis code L455F for 2WD or L465F for 4WD compatibility before ordering
Enter your registration above for instant DVLA-verified engine code confirmation
KF-VE and KF-DET codes require specialist verification for correct replacement matching
items.cta: Verify your Subaru Lucra KF engine code instantly via DVLA lookup

items.question: Is a reconditioned Subaru Lucra engine better than a used Japanese import?
items.answer: Reconditioned KF engines offer UK warranty coverage and upgraded components addressing known failures like injector wear, while used Japanese imports provide lower upfront cost but limited warranty and potential compliance documentation gaps.
keyPoints: Reconditioned units include 12-month UK warranty vs 3-6 months for Japanese imports
Rebuilt KF engines feature upgraded injectors addressing Lucra failure patterns
Japanese imports require additional IVA/MOT documentation verification for UK compliance
Budget-conscious owners may prefer imports; long-term owners benefit from reconditioned reliability

items.question: Will a replacement engine affect my Subaru Lucra UK MOT or registration?
items.answer: Replacement engines must meet UK emissions standards and include proper documentation for DVLA registration; EngineMarket specialists supply KF units with Euro 4 certification and IVA support for grey import compliance.
keyPoints: All replacement KF engines include UK-spec catalytic converter for MOT emissions compliance
Emissions certification documentation provided for DVLA registration updates on grey imports
IVA test support included for Lucra vehicles requiring Individual Vehicle Approval
Specialists handle ECU immobiliser adaptation and CVT calibration for seamless UK registration transfer

items.question: How long does Subaru Lucra engine replacement take in the UK?
items.answer: UK reconditioned KF engines typically dispatch within 2 days; Japanese imports require 10-14 days delivery plus 1 day fitting. Supply & fit bookings can often be scheduled within 48 hours for in-stock units.
keyPoints: In-stock UK reconditioned units: 2-day supply & fit turnaround typical for KF variants
Japanese import engines: 10-14 days delivery plus 1 day specialist fitting with chassis verification
CVT and AWD variants may require additional time for adaptation service and drivetrain compatibility checks
Emergency replacement helpline available for seized engine scenarios with priority dispatch from UK stock

items.question: Can I fit a Subaru Lucra engine myself to save money?
items.answer: DIY engine fitting is not recommended for Subaru Lucra grey imports due to kei car-specific tools, CVT adaptation requirements, AWD drivetrain calibration and UK compliance documentation best handled by specialists.
keyPoints: KF-series engines require specific lifting equipment and timing tools for safe installation
CVT variants need ECU adaptation service - specialist tooling essential for smooth operation
IVA/MOT documentation support requires specialist knowledge of grey import compliance procedures
Supply & fit packages often cost less than DIY when factoring tool hire, risk of errors and warranty validity
items.cta: Book Subaru Lucra engine supply & fit with grey import specialist network

items.question: What are the most common Subaru Lucra engine problems?
items.answer: KF injector failure causing rough idle and stalling, coolant sensor connector corrosion leading to ECU errors, and MAF sensor degradation causing rich mixture dominate Subaru Lucra replacement enquiries from UK grey import owners.
keyPoints: Injector failure affects KF-VE and KF-DET variants; rebuilt units include upgraded injectors as standard
Coolant sensor connector corrosion common on pre-2012 units; revised connectors available on rebuilt engines
MAF sensor degradation causes black spark plugs and emissions test failures on urban-driven imports
All rebuilt KF units include upgraded components addressing these known grey import failure points

items.question: Is it worth repairing or replacing my Subaru Lucra engine?
items.answer: If repair costs exceed 60% of a reconditioned KF price (£1,500-£2,200), replacement offers better long-term value with UK warranty coverage and upgraded components addressing original failure patterns.
keyPoints: Minor repairs (injector, sensor replacement) cost £180-£480 and may be viable for low-mileage units
Major failures (multiple injector failure, overheating damage) typically require full replacement for reliability
Rebuilt KF engines include 12-month warranty vs limited coverage on repaired original units
Consider vehicle value: Lucra grey imports typically worth £2,000-£4,000 in UK market

items.question: Does the Subaru Lucra engine work with Daihatsu donor parts?
items.answer: Yes - the KF engine was also fitted to Daihatsu Tanto Exe in other markets, expanding UK parts availability and reducing lead times for grey import Lucra owners seeking replacement components.

Injectors, sensors and gaskets often available from Daihatsu Tanto Exe suppliers in UK
Verify part numbers match your Lucra chassis code (L455F/L465F) and variant before ordering
EngineMarket specialists source from cross-platform Daihatsu inventory to reduce wait times
